folio.label: 44r
folio.l: Er that he mygút gete , his wyf to shipe
Hym had be leuere , I dar wel vndertake
At thilk tyme , than alle hise wetheres blake
That she had had , a ship~ hire self a lone
And æäfore wost æè what , is best to done
This asketh hast , and of an hasty thyng
Men mounö nat päche , and maken tariyng
A non go gete vs fattes , in to this Inne
A knedyng trow , or ellis a kamelyne
For ecú of vs , but loke æ they be large
In whiche we mounö swy~me , as in a barge
And haue æäe Inne , vetaille sufficient
But for o day , fy on the remenaunt
The water shal a slake , and gon a wey
A boute prime , vp on the next day
But Robyn may nat wyte , of this , thy knaue
Ne eke thy mayde Gille , I may nat saue
Aske nat why , for thougú æè axe me
I wol nat telle , goddis preuyte
Suffiseth the , but if thyne wyttes madde
To han as gret a grace , as Noe hadde
Thy wyf shal I wel sauen , ou©t of doute
Go now thy wey , and spede the here a boute
But whan æè hast , for hire and the and me
I geten vs , theise knedyng tubbes thre
Tha~ne shalt æè hangen hem , in the roof ful heye
That noman , of oure purueance , espie
And whan æow hast don~ , as I haue seid
And hast oure vetaille , faire in hem leyd
And eke an ax , to smyte the corde a two
Whan æ the water cometh , æ we may go
And breke an hole an hey , vp on the gable
Vn to the gardeyn ward , ouer the stable
That we mounö freely passen , forth oure wey
Wha~ne æ the grete shour , is gon a wey
Tha~ne shalt æè swy~me , as mery I vndertake
As doth the white doke , after hire drake
Than wyl I clepe , how Alisou~ , how Ioún
Be mery , for the flode , shal passe a nonö
And æè wolt seyn , heyl Maister Nicholay
Good morwe , I se the wele , for it is day
And æa~ne shuln we , be lordes aÂ oure lyf
Of al the world , as Noe and his wyf

note.notetxt: 396Last four lines are written with a different pen, a lighter ink perhaps, but the lines are accomodated well in the space left before the lower ruling line, no compression.ODR
